name: Jane | Benton Grove High
description:
She stole $150 from her mother.
Bought a toy railroad.
Carries weed and a taser in her pocket.
Gives you a thumbs-up on the Iowan Highway.

Will you stop?

⛛ ⛛ ⛛

Jane’s story serves as the intro to my magnum opus: a collective portrait of twenty-one young women from Benton Ridge, a small town in Iowa.

Set in the final months of high school, these stories capture a fragile, liminal period — when one era is ending and another has yet to begin. The girls come from different households, economies, and silences. Each carries her own measure of strength and vulnerability, whether she recognizes it or not.




⛛ ⛛ ⛛

▸You begin as a stranger with no prior acquaintance with the character. Any bond, whether platonic or romantic, must be earned organically. This is a very cold, potentially difficult start.

▸Conceptually it's a slow burn.

▸{{user}} should begin their first message with a short description of their car. Focus on what Jane would notice first: color, model, condition, stickers, music leaking out, etc. This approach lets Jane's response flow naturally from observation to interaction.

▸This experience is designed for advanced models. JLLM will struggle.

▸The prompt remains openly available for reference or those who are especially curious.

▸However, Jane is intended to be explored blind on a first run.

▸Each girl is series accompanied by her own dedicated support lorebook, containing her subjective opinions of other characters (including unreleased) + crisis dynamics.



☕︎ You’re welcome to make private copies/branches for personal use (here or on other platforms, where you have access to better models) or adapt my templates as you like. This said, I’d appreciate it if you could spend a little time with this ''main release'' — visibility is a challenge for niche creators like me, every bit of engagement really helps.


🙽 Other bots in the series: I'll link them as they're released:

Intro: Jane

Season 1: Circe,
